data.title: Kosmera to unveil first prototype hypercar at CES 2026 in Las Vegas from Jan. 6-9, redefining intelligent mobility.
data.description: A company called Kosmera, focused on advancing intelligent mobility, revealed that their inaugural prototype, an energy-efficient hypercar, will premiere at the 2026 Consumer Electronics Show (CES) in Las Vegas from January 6–9.
BoC's Minutes: Governing council prepared to respond in case of a major new shock, or data showing economy and inflation diverging materially from the outlook.
BoC's Minutes: The governing council agreed to remain cautious in interpreting data, given recent volatility.
BoC Minutes: The governing council noted that despite the risk that higher costs from trade disruption could spill over into consumer prices, the effects have been limited so far.
BOC Minutes: Governing council concluded that this could be because of slower growth in unit labour costs.
BoC Minutes: Governing council felt uncertainty leading up to and during USMCA talks would likely weigh on business investment.
BoC's Minutes: Volatility in recent quarterly GDP readings underlines how challenging it will be to judge underlying economic trends.
BoC's Minutes: The governing council agreed that the upcoming review of the USMCA trade treaty was a significant risk.

data.title: Sintana Energy Inc. (TSX-V: SEI, AIM: SEI, OTCQX: SEUSF) announces admission to trading on AIM in London market.
data.description: Sintana Energy Inc. has recently shared the news of its common shares (TSX-V: SEI, AIM: SEI, OTCQX: SEUSF) being admitted for trading on the AIM market of the London Stock Exchange plc. This move marks a significant step for the company, allowing its Common Shares to be traded on AIM. The development is expected to enhance visibility and accessibility for investors interested in Sintana, demonstrating the company's growth and potential in the market. This strategic decision aligns with the company's objectives for expansion and increasing shareholder value.

data.title: Silver breaks above $70, Novo Nordisk surges 9% on obesity pill approval. Wall Street rises, S&P 500 at 6,900, with 4.3% US GDP growth in Q3. Fed easing expectations decrease to 15%.
data.description: On Wednesday, Wall Street saw modest gains as investors anticipated a strong year-end period. By midday in New York, the S&P 500 rose to around 6,900, nearing October’s record of 6,920. The Nasdaq 100 also increased by 0.4% while the Dow Jones Industrial Average went up by 0.3%. However, small caps did not perform as well, with the Russell 2000 down by approximately 0.6%. The day was influenced by the release of better-than-expected U.S. GDP data. In the third quarter, the GDP grew at a 4.3% annualized rate, surpassing the estimated 3.3% and being the quickest growth in two years. As a result, traders adjusted their expectations for Federal Reserve easing. There is now a 15% likelihood of a 25-basis-point cut in late January, reduced from the previous day’s 20%.

data.title: Wilmington's new TV series Coastal Connection added to REAL Shows Network, focusing on real estate and lifestyle in the city.
data.description: In Wilmington, North Carolina, on December 23, 2025, REAL Shows Network (RSN), a national TV channel focused on real estate and lifestyle narratives, introduced Coastal Connection as part of its programming lineup. The show, hosted by a prominent figure in the local real estate sector, aims to showcase the unique lifestyle and property offerings in the region.

data.title: Analyst trims Meta stock price target, expects wounded sentiment until 2026. Will it recover? #Investors #Meta #Stocks
data.description: Analysts at Baird recommended buying Meta stock, although they anticipate continued negative sentiment for the social media company through 2026. They have reduced the price target for Meta stock. The possibility of a recovery in sentiment is questioned for the year 2026.

data.title: Halozyme Therapeutics stock shows strength, upgraded IBD Relative Strength Rating due to improving price performance.
data.description: Halozyme Therapeutics has demonstrated positive improvement in its price performance, leading to an upgrade in its IBD Relative Strength Rating. This upgrade reflects the company's strengthening position in the market. This enhancement in rating signifies the potential for increased growth and stability in Halozyme Therapeutics' stock.

data.title: Applied Optoelectronics stock sees price performance increase, earns upgrade to IBD Relative Strength Rating.
data.description: Applied Optoelectronics has demonstrated an increase in price performance, resulting in an upgrade to its IBD Relative Strength Rating. This upgrade showcases the company's strong performance in the market.
